It further requires districts to adopt social media policies generally prohibiting student access to social media platforms through school-owned equipment and services.

name: An Act Relating to Children
description: This bill requires entities that offer online products, services, or features likely to be accessed by children to complete data protection impact assessments, configure default privacy settings to a high level of privacy, and act in the best interest of children when processing their personal data. It further prohibits practices such as default profiling, collection of unnecessary or precise geolocation data, and use of dark patterns.

name: Minor access and use of social media
description: This bill requires social media operators to obtain verifiable parental consent before allowing a minor (under 16) to view social media. It further requires companies to establish procedures for identifying minors' accounts, securing collected information through encryption, and allowing parents to revoke consent.

name: An Act Concerning Minors And Social Media
description: This bill prohibits any social media algorithm that targets individuals younger than 17 years of age, including by tracking such individual's age or telephone number, and to prohibit inclusion of a continuous feed in the social media account of any individual younger than 17.

name: Restrict Use of Social Media During Instructional Time & Prohibit Access
description: This bill requires school district and charter school boards to adopt Internet safety policies, prohibit and prevent student access to social media platforms through school-provided internet access (except when directed by a teacher for educational purposes), and ban use of TikTok or successor platforms on district-owned devices and networks. It further directs the State Department of Education to implement a Grades 6-12 curriculum on the social, emotional, and physical effects of social media, and amends student codes of conduct to restrict wireless communications device use during instructional time.

name: Child Data Privacy and Protection Act
description: This bill establishes the "Child Data Privacy and Protection Act," which requires entities offering online products targeted toward child users to complete data protection impact assessments, restricts collection, retention, processing, or sale of children's personal data to what is necessary, and prohibits targeted digital advertising to children without parental consent. It further mandates privacy by default, prominent privacy policies and emergency-notification methods,and parental access to a deceased child's account.

name: Age-Appropriate Design Code Act
description: This bill establishes the "South Carolina Age-Appropriate Design Code Act,"  which requires entities meeting specified revenue or data-processing thresholds to act in the best interests of children, complete data protection impact assessments for online services reasonably likely to be accessed by children, and configure default privacy settings to a high level of privacy. It prohibits practices such as default profiling, processing unnecessary or precise geolocation data, and using dark patterns.

name: Social Media Platform Owners or Operators
description: This bill requires owners or operators of social media platforms that contract with Mississippi users to ensure content policies address risks to youth related to tobacco or nicotine products, including content linking to sites that sell such products without age verification and content promoting their harmful misuse. It further requires platforms to provide a reporting mechanism for such content and to have procedures for evaluating and removing it.

name: Walker Montgomery Protecting Children Online Act
description: This bill establishes the "Walker Montgomery Protecting Children Online Act" which requires social media providers to verify users' ages, obtain parental consent for known minors, limit collection/use and sharing of minors' personal data, and implement strategies to mitigate minors' exposure to harmful content. It further amends Mississippi's child exploitation law to expand the definition of prohibited material to include morphed or digitally altered images depicting identifiable minors in sexually explicit conduct.

name: Social Media Depicting Tobacco Misuse
description: This bill requires owners or operators of social media platforms that contract with Mississippi users to ensure content policies address risks to youth related to tobacco or nicotine products, including content linking to sites that sell such products without age verification and content promoting their harmful misuse. It further requires platforms to provide a reporting mechanism for such content and to have procedures for evaluating and removing it.

name: Protection of Children Using Social Media
description: This bill requires digital service providers to verify users' ages, limits the collection, use, and sharing of known minors' personal information, and prohibits targeted advertising, financial transactions, and precise geolocation tracking for known minors absent caregiver permission. It also requires providers to implement controls preventing minors' exposure to obscene material, provide caregivers with supervisory tools, and verify caregiver identity and relationship to the minor.

name: Internet Safety Instruction for Children
description: This bill requires local school districts to prohibit students from accessing social media platforms unless directed by a teacher for educational purposes, and to establish a social media safety policy addressing topics such as time management, mental health effects, manipulation tactics, and identifying cyberbullying and predatory behavior. It also requires districts to provide annual social media safety instruction to students in grades 6-12.

name: Sex Offenders
description: This bill prohibits a registered sex offender who committed an offense against a minor victim from creating or controlling a social media account unless the account displays the registrant's full legal name, and applies this requirement retroactively.

name: Protection of Children and Internet Usage
description: This bill requires covered entities offering online services likely to be accessed by children to act in children's best interests by completing data protection impact assessments, configuring default privacy settings to a high level, providing notice when children are monitored or tracked, and refraining from harmful data processing, profiling, or use of dark patterns.

name: Social media; categorizing social media platforms as addictive
description: This bill defines "social media platform" and declares that social media platforms shall be deemed addictive and dangerous to mental health, especially that of minors.

name: A Bill for an Act Relating to Offenses
description: This bill creates the offense of digital grooming, making it a Class IV felony for a person 19 or older to knowingly use an electronic communication device or social media to communicate with a child 16 or younger with intent to engage in sexual contact, obtain sexual images, or extort the child.

name: An Act Concerning Minors And Digital Privacy.
description: This bill amends the general statutes to prohibit the collection and commercial use of digital consumer data and personally identifying information concerning minors.

name: Securing Children Online through Parental Empowerment (SCOPE) Act
description: This bill introduces the SCOPE Act which requires social media and similar services to verify user ages, restrict data collection on known minors, block harmful content, and give parents supervisory tools over their children's accounts. It also requires public schools to install internet filters on devices given to students and mandates a legislative study on the effects of media on minors.

name: prohibiting use of social media platforms by children
description: This bill prohibits anyone under 18 from using social media platforms and requires platforms to verify that all users are at least 18 years old. Further, it allows parents to request the deletion of their child's account and associated personal data within 10 days, with violations enforceable by the state attorney general.

name: prohibiting targeting of minors by algorithms
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using algorithms to push user-generated content to minors and holds them liable if they knowingly do so.

name: social media access on public school campuses
description: This bill requires public schools to disable access to social media sites on devices connected to school internet.

name: relating to agreements between minors and digital services
description: This bill requires digital service providers (social media apps, websites, etc.) to obtain verifiable parental consent before entering into an agreement with a minor ages 13 to 17 to use their service.

name: sexual material harmful to minors
description: This bill requires sites with sexual content harmful to minors under the age of 18 to require age verification, restricts data gathering and creates liability for any violations of the agreement.

name: Relating to prohibiting use of social media platforms by children.
description: This bill prohibits children under 18 from using social media platforms and requires platforms to verify that account holders are 18 or older using a commercially reasonable method, while limiting the retention and use of personal information collected for verification. It also requires platforms to delete a child's account within 10 days of a verified parental request

name: Warning label on social media
description: This bill requires social media platforms operating in Texas to display a warning label, each time a user opens the platform, concerning the association between a minor's social media usage and significant mental health issues, and to prevent the user from operating the platform until they verify they understand the warning.

name: Age Verification
description: This bill requires social media platforms to verify the age of account holders on the platform. Further, it allows parents to request termination of their child's account.

name: App Store Age Verification
description: This bill provides regulation of platforms for the sale and distribution of apps for mobile devices including age verification, parental consent, and data protection.

name: No phones in schools
description: This bill requires schools to adopt a policy prohibiting a student from using a personal communication device while on school property during the school day.

name: An act relating to instruction in online safety for minors provided in public schools and by the Department of Public Safety.
description: This bill requires Texas public schools to offer online safety instruction as part of the enrichment curriculum, covering the dangers of social media and AI applications, personal security, oversharing, cyberbullying, predatory behavior, and human trafficking. It also directs the Department of Public Safety to include online safety education for citizens under 18 in its public safety and crime prevention plan.

name: Relating to prohibiting use of social media platforms by children.
description: This bill prohibits minors from opening social media accounts. Further, social media platforms must check users' ages with government-issued identification. This bill also requires telecommunications providers to put filters on devices ensuring that minors do not share their location or access explicit material. This bill additionally prohibits the use of cell phones during school days.

name: Relating to the creation of artificial sexual material harmful to minors
description: This bill requires a commercial entity that operates an Internet website with a publicly accessible tool for creating artificial sexual material harmful to minors or otherwise makes publicly available an application for creating sexual material harmful to minors to use reasonable age verification methods to verify an individual attempting to access the tool is 18 years of age or older. Further, the entity must ensure that an individual used as a source for the material is 18 years of age or older and has consented to the use of the individual's face and body as a source for the material.

name: Minor Social Media Users
description: This bill prohibits social media companies from allowing individuals younger than 18 to maintain a social media account, subjecting violating companies to a civil penalty of $1,000 per violation. It authorizes the attorney general to bring a civil enforcement action on behalf of an affected minor.

name: Social Media Parental Authorization Act
description: This bill requires social media companies to obtain prior parental authorization before permitting a minor to create an account, prohibits data collection on minors absent such authorization, and requires companies to give consenting parents tools to view and monitor the minor's account, posts, and messages.

name: An act relating to the requirements for chatbot deployers, including required protocols, limitations on data collection, and requirements for minors to interact with artificial intelligence companions and therapeutic chatbots, and providing civil penalties, punitive penalties, and civil causes of action.
description: This bill requires chatbot deployers to implement protocols to detect and mitigate harm to users and limit data collection, and mandates reasonable age verification to prevent minors from using AI companions designed to simulate romantic or emotional bonds. It restricts therapeutic chatbots for minors to those recommended by a licensed professional, supported by clinical trial data, and accompanied by clear disclosures and safety protocols, with enforcement by the attorney general and a private right of action for minors harmed by noncompliant chatbots.

name: An act modifying the duties of the workforce development board, state board of education, and department of education related to cyber and artificial intelligence
description: This bill directs the workforce development board to establish a comprehensive initiative addressing the state's cyber and artificial intelligence workforce needs, including K-12 literacy standards, higher education partnerships, and grant and career transition programs. It also requires the state board of education and department of education to develop statewide cyber and AI educational frameworks and standards, a voluntary school certification program, and a community education initiative for cyber and AI awareness targeting demographics including the elderly, minors, and educators.

name: Age verification and Parental Consent
description: This bill requires app store operators to estimate a device's primary user's age, obtain parental consent before a known child downloads an application, and provide developers with a real-time age signal, while requiring developers with knowledge that a user is under 18 to provide parental controls and content/time-management features.

name: Child Online Protection and Liability
description: This bill requires social media companies to obtain parental consent and verify the age of West Virginia minor account holders, restricts minors' accounts from direct messaging with non-friends, search visibility, advertising, targeted content, and data collection, and limits minors' access to overnight hours unless adjusted by a consenting parent who is given account monitoring access. It also prohibits platform designs known to cause addiction in minors.

name: Social Media Safety Act
description: This bill creates the Social Media Safety Act, requiring social media companies to use a third-party vendor to perform reasonable age verification before allowing access to their platform and to obtain express parental or guardian consent before allowing a minor to become an account holder. It also prohibits social media companies, commercial entities, and third-party vendors from retaining individuals' identifying information after access has been granted,

name: Act 122
description: This bill creates the "Bell to Bell, No Cell Act," requiring each public school district and open-enrollment public charter school to establish, by the 2025-2026 school year, a written student discipline policy that restricts or prohibits possession and use of personal electronic devices during the school day, subject to certain exemptions, and to submit the policy to the Division of Elementary and Secondary Education for approval.

name: Children And Teens' Online Privacy Protection Act
description: This bill creates the Arkansas Children and Teens' Online Privacy Protection Act, prohibiting operators of websites or applications directed at children or teens, or with actual knowledge they are collecting such users' personal information, from using that information for targeted advertising, collecting it beyond what is consistent with the service or required by law, or retaining it longer than reasonably necessary, while also requiring clear notice, parental or teen consent, and the ability to access, correct, or delete the information.

name: To Amend The Social Media Safety Act.
description: This bill amends the Social Media Safety Act by lowering the age threshold for "minor" and "reasonable age verification" from 18 to 16, expanding the definitions of "social media platform" and related terms, and requiring covered platforms to limit addictive design features, restrict notifications to minors between 10:00 p.m. and 6:00 a.m., default to the most protective privacy settings, conduct quarterly compliance audits, and provide parents a dashboard to monitor and restrict their child's use.

name: To Create A Private Right Of Action Against A Social Media Platform That Causes Harm; And To Impose A Civil Penalty On A Social Media Platform That Knowingly And Willfully Contributes To A Minor's Suicide Or Suicide Attempt.
description: This bill creates a private right of action against social media platforms that use a design, algorithm, or feature they know or should know causes a user to purchase controlled substances, develop an eating disorder, attempt suicide, or develop an addiction to the platform, and allows a parent or guardian whose minor child commits or attempts suicide following exposure to self-harm or suicide content to sue the platform that facilitated the connection to that content.

name: Child Content Creation Protection Act
description: This bill creates the Child Content Creation Protection Act, requiring content creators who feature a minor meeting certain compensation and content thresholds to maintain records of the minor's involvement, compensate the minor, and set aside a portion of earnings in a trust account preserved until the minor turns 18.

name: contract with minors
description: This bill enacts R.S. 9:2717.1, prohibiting an interactive computer service from entering into a contract or agreement, including creating an online account, with a minor without the consent of the minor's legal representative, and provides that any such contract entered into without that consent is a relative nullity.

name: Secure Online Interaction and Age Limitation Act
description: This bill creates the Secure Online Child Interaction and Age Limitation Act, requiring social media companies to make commercially reasonable efforts to verify users' ages and to obtain express parental or guardian consent before allowing a Louisiana resident under 16 to hold an account, while also prohibiting unconnected adults from direct messaging minor account holders, restricting targeted advertising and data collection involving minors, and requiring platforms to provide parents tools to supervise a minor's account settings, time limits, and notifications.

name: Louisiana Kids Online Protection and Anti‑Grooming Act
description: This bill enacts the Kids Online Protection and Anti-Grooming Act, establishing a duty of care for covered platforms that contract with minors, requiring default privacy settings that prohibit adults from connecting with or directly messaging a minor without the minor's connection or legal representative's consent, bar disclosure of a minor's precise geolocation to non-guardians, restrict account visibility to connected accounts, and notify a legal representative when a minor is exposed to sexually explicit material or connects with another user. It also requires platforms to provide parental supervision tools to manage settings, view and block connected accounts, and limit microtransactions.

name: Mandatory Age verification
description: This bill requires covered application store providers to verify users' age category at account creation and, for minors, to require affiliation with a parent account and obtain verifiable parental consent before allowing app downloads, purchases, or in-app purchases, while requiring developers to verify age category data shared by the application store and similarly obtain parental consent for minor accounts before such transactions.

name: Guidelines for User Age-Verification and Responsible Dialogue Act
description: This bill enacts the Guidelines for User Age-Verification and Responsible Dialogue Act, criminalizing the knowing or reckless design, development, or deployment of an AI chatbot capable of soliciting a minor into sexually explicit conduct, creating sexually explicit depictions involving a minor, or encouraging a minor toward suicide, self-harm, or violence.

name: Provides relative to the duty of care for online platforms that contract with minors
description: This bill amends the Kids Online Protection and Anti-Grooming Act by replacing the term "sexually explicit material" with "material harmful to minors" (cross-referencing R.S. 51:2121) and updating the definition of "minor" to align with R.S. 9:2717.2, along with other technical and conforming changes to definitions. It also revises the required default privacy settings for minors, allowing a legal representative to either consent to adult connections or instead access a list of the minor's connections and direct-message contacts, and changing the visibility restriction so minor accounts are set to private mode where only connected users can view or respond to the minor's posted content.

name: Social Media Use for Minors
description: This bill prohibits minors under 14 from holding social media accounts and requires platforms to terminate such accounts, while minors aged 14-15 may hold accounts only with parental consent; it also requires platforms to honor termination requests from minors or their confirmed parents/guardians and permanently delete the terminated account's personal information.

name: Social media usage amendments
description: This bill enacts the Utah Social Media Regulation Act, which prohibits a social media company from using a design or feature that it knows, or reasonably should know, causes a minor to have an addiction to its platform, and grants the Division of Consumer Protection authority to audit compliance and impose civil penalties of up to $250,000 per addictive practice plus up to $2,500 per affected minor.

name: Digital wellness, citizenship, and safe technology commission
description: This bill creates the Digital Wellness, Citizenship, and Safe Technology Commission, an 11-member body including legislators, the governor, the attorney general, and digital citizenship experts, tasked with identifying best practices and compiling resources to train every student, parent, and support network in digital citizenship and safe technology use.

name: Child Actor Regulations
description: This bill regulates the employment of minors in entertainment and social media content, requiring that traditional performers earning $20,000 or more annually have 15% of gross earnings placed in a trust, and that "qualifying minors" featured in a content creator's social media receive a calculated share of earnings via a statutory formula, also placed in trust until age 18.

name: App Store Accountability Act
description: This bill, the App Store Accountability Act, requires app store providers to verify users' age category, require minor accounts to be affiliated with a parent account, and obtain verifiable parental consent before minors can download apps, make purchases, or be subject to significant changes to terms or content.

name: Online Age Verification Amendments
description: This bill imposes a 2% excise tax on commercial entities required to perform age verification for online material harmful to minors, with 90% of revenue funding mental health treatment, education, and prevention programs for minors through a new Minor Mental Health Restricted Account, and 10% funding age-verification enforcement through a new Minor Online Safety Restricted Account.

name: Artifical Intelligence Amendments
description: This bill enacts the Companion Chatbot Safety Act, requiring operators of AI companion chatbots to comply with the Utah Consumer Privacy Act, prevent chatbots from generating responses that encourage suicide or self-harm, refer distressed users to crisis resources, and clearly label advertisements. For minor users, operators must provide periodic break reminders and AI-disclosure notices, opt minors out of targeted advertising by default, avoid collecting unnecessary data or directing minors to harmful material, take reasonable steps to prevent chatbots from encouraging substance use, self-harm, disordered eating, or other risky behavior, and give parents tools to control data retention, set time limits, and disable access.

name: Educational Technology Regulatory Sandbox
description: This bill creates an Educational Technology Regulatory Sandbox within the public education system, establishing minimum standards for artificial intelligence use in schools.

name: Creating the Joint Interim Study Commission for Protecting Children from Internet Pornography
description: This bill creates the Joint Interim Study Commission on Protecting Children from Internet Pornography, establishes the membership of the study commission, and requires the study commission to draft a legislative proposal to protect children accessing the internet, including preventing children from accessing internet pornography, safeguarding children using social media, and addressing related mental health and cybersecurity issues.

name: Act relating to social media
description: This bill requires social media platforms to use a commercially reasonable age verification process, terminate accounts upon request, display time-use notifications and provide tools and settings for parents and minors to manage minors' accounts, and prohibit certain actions regarding minors' accounts.

name: Relating to consumer protection; to require covered platform operators to restrict access to certain portions of platforms unless the age of the user is determined or parental consent is obtained; to limit the hours covered operators may send notifications to covered minors; to require covered operators to use default platform settings on all covered platforms that limit covered minors' access, notifications, and engagement during certain hours; to require covered operators to establish and maintain a mechanism permitting parents and legal guardians to require more stringent settings on covered platforms; to require covered operators to annually disclose certain data; and to make a violation of its provisions a violation of the Alabama Deceptive Trade Practices Act.
description: This bill requires covered platform operators to restrict minors' access to portions of platforms that recommend, select, or prioritize media items based on personal information unless the user is verified as not a minor or parental consent is obtained, limits the hours covered operators may send notifications to covered minors, requires default platform settings restricting minors' access and engagement during certain time periods, requires a parental mechanism to impose more stringent settings, and requires covered operators to annually disclose certain usage data.

name: Relating to artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots; to require AI chatbot deployers to implement a reasonable age verification process and verify the age of all AI chatbot users; to provide  prohibitions on the provision of certain AI chatbots to minors; to require AI chatbot deployers to provide alternative versions of the platform without human-like features to minors; to require AI chatbot deployers to adopt protocols for AI chatbots to detect and mitigate emergency situations; to limit the amount and type of information AI chatbot deployers are allowed to collect and store; to allow therapeutic AI chatbots meeting certain requirements to be made available to minors; to provide a private right of action for certain users; and to authorize the Attorney General to bring suit to enforce this act.
description: This bill requires AI chatbot deployers to implement a reasonable age verification process, verify the age of each user, and ensure that AI chatbots with human-like features are not made available to minors unless an alternative version without such features is provided, requires deployers to adopt protocols to detect and mitigate emergency situations involving self-harm or harm to others, limits the information deployers may collect and store, allows therapeutic AI chatbots meeting certain requirements to be prescribed by a licensed mental health professional for use by minors.

name: Georgia Child Performer Empowerment and Protection Act
description: This bill establishes the "Georgia Child Performer Empowerment and Protection Act," which requires the establishment of blocked trust accounts for minors rendering artistic or creative services, including minors engaged in online content creation

name: Impact of Social Media and Artificial Intelligence Committee
description: This bill establishes the Senate Impact of Social Media and Artificial Intelligence on Children and Platform Privacy Protection Study Committee. The committee will evaluate the impact social media usage and artificial intelligence has on children and determine how existing online privacy protections may be improved.

name: Online Internet Safety
description: This bill requires social media platforms to implement age verification. Additionally, this bill places restrictions on account activity for minors under the age of 16, including direct messaging with adults, advertising using personal information, and data collection.

name: Protecting Children's Mental Health
description: This bill requires commercial entities that operate a social media platform to implement age verification to restrict access minors under the age of 16.

name: Protecting Georgia's Children on Social Media Act of 2024
description: This bill establishes the "Protecting Georgia's Children on Social Media Act of 2024," which requires social media platforms to verify users' ages, obtain parental consent before allowing minors under 16 to hold accounts, and restrict targeted advertising and data collection for minor account holders.

name: Internet Safety Advisory Council
description: This bill creates a council to review and recommend policies for public elementary and secondary schools regarding social media and technology use for students.

name: Social Media Platforms for Minors
description: This bill requires social media platforms to use methods such as a neutral age screen mechanism to determine whether a user is a minor under the age of 16. This bill also requires social media platforms to limit a minor's use to one hour per day, per service or application, and allow parents to increase or decrease the daily time limit.

name: Commericial Entity Offering Social Media Accounts
description: This bill requires social media companies to implement a curfew prohibiting minors from accessing the platform between 12 AM and 6 AM unless the minor has parental consent.

name: Consumer Data Protection Act
description: This bill places strict limitations on how a controller can process personal data collected from a minor under the age of 13 using its platform. This includes restrictions on targeted advertising, the sale of personal data, and the use of geolocation data.

name: Consumer Data Protection Act
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using certain designs and features (such as infinite scroll, auto-playing videos, push notifications, and virtual gifts) for users who are under the age of 18.

name: Consumer Data Protection Act
description: This bill amends Virginia's Consumer Data Protection Act to require social media platforms subject to the federal Children's Online Privacy Protection Act to obtain verifiable parental consent before permitting a minor to create or use an account, while giving parents the option to consent to collection and use of the minor's personal data without consenting to its disclosure to third parties.

name: Consumer Data Protection Act
description: This bill restricts access to TikTok for minors under the age of 18.

name: Employment of Children
description: This bill regulates vlogging (video blogging) featuring minors by requiring vloggers whose compensated video content meets specified thresholds involving a minor's likeness, name, or photograph to set aside a percentage o earnings in a trust account for the minor's benefit upon turning 18. It further establishes a "right to be forgotten," allowing individuals who appeared as minors in monetized video content to request permanent deletion of that content from social media or internet platforms once they turn 18.

name: SOCIAL MEDIA SAFETY FOR MINORS
description: This bill requires age verification of social media accounts, prohibiting accounts for those under 14 and requiring easily revocable parental consent for accounts of children 14-15 years old.

name: Study the Effects of Artificial Intelligence, Cellular Telephones and Social Media on Public Education
description: This bill directs the Department of Education to establish a working group to study the use of artificial intelligence, cellular telephones, and social media in public school settings and to submit a report by December 3, 2025, with findings and recommendations, including suggested legislation, to the Joint Standing Committee on Education and Cultural Affairs.

name: Stop Harms from Addictive Social Media Act
description: This bill requires parental consent for the creation of a social media account by anyone under the age of 16. It also explicitly bans addictive interface features including, but not limited to, infinite scrolling, push notifications regarding new content or responses,  auto-play video, and displaying of "likes." It also forbids "profile-based" feeds and advertising, that is, feeds and advertising based on the user's personal information and not due to their direct actions such as following or liking a page or post.

name: Distraction-Free Learning Policy
description: This bill requires school districts to adopt policies limiting students' use of electronic communications devices while at school.

name: Commission on Effects of Social Media on Adolescents
description: This bill establishes the Commission on the Effects of Social Media Usage on Adolescents.  The purpose of the commission is to study the extent of social media usage in and out of public schools and to determine the effects that use has on the health and academic performance of students.

name: S3695
description: This bill requires the Commissioner of Education to develop guidelines, in consultation with education stakeholders, governing student use of internet-enabled devices during school hours, on school buses, and at school-sanctioned events, including age-appropriate restrictions, exceptions for emergencies, health needs, translation, and student caregivers, and guidance on device storage and network-based restrictions on social media access.

name: Endangering Welfare of Children
description: This bill amends New Jersey's child endangerment statute to make it a crime to knowingly act, via electronic communication such as social media, in a manner likely to be injurious to a child's physical, mental, or moral welfare.

name: Endangering Welfare of Children
description: This bill amends New Jersey's child endangerment statute to make it a crime to knowingly act, via electronic communication such as social media, in a manner likely to be injurious to a child's physical, mental, or moral welfare.

name: Endangering Welfare of Children
description: This bill amends New Jersey's child endangerment statute to make it a crime to knowingly act, via electronic communication such as social media, in a manner likely to be injurious to a child's physical, mental, or moral welfare.

name: A4919
description: This bill requires social media companies to complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment when launching a new service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children. This will document privacy risks, designate an age level that the feature is appropriate for, set privacy settings to the maximum for children, provide privacy information in age appropriate language to children, and assess the potential impact of the feature on children. This bill also establishes the New Jersey Children’s Data Protection Commission to oversee and monitor the impact of social media on children, and provide guidance and direction to the state government.

name: Social Media Privacy and Data Standards for Children
description: This bill requires social media companies to complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment when launching a new service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children. This will document privacy risks, designate an age level that the feature is appropriate for, set privacy settings to the maximum for children, provide privacy information in age appropriate language to children, and assess the potential impact of the feature on children. This bill also establishes the New Jersey Children’s Data Protection Commission to oversee and monitor the impact of social media on children, and provide guidance and direction to the state government.

name: A1879
description: This bill requires social media companies to complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment when launching a new service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children. This will document privacy risks, designate an age level that the feature is appropriate for, set privacy settings to the maximum for children, provide privacy information in age appropriate language to children, and assess the potential impact of the feature on children. This bill also establishes the New Jersey Children’s Data Protection Commission to oversee and monitor the impact of social media on children, and provide guidance and direction to the state government.

name: A1358
description: This bill requires social media companies to complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment when launching a new service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children. This will document privacy risks, designate an age level that the feature is appropriate for, set privacy settings to the maximum for children, provide privacy information in age appropriate language to children, and assess the potential impact of the feature on children. This bill also establishes the New Jersey Children’s Data Protection Commission to oversee and monitor the impact of social media on children, and provide guidance and direction to the state government.

name: Online Children's Safety Protection Act
description: This bill establishes the Online Children's Safety Protection Act, requiring covered entities that provide an online service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children to complete a data protection impact assessment determining whether the service is designed and offered consistent with the best interests of the child, and prohibits practices such as profiling, collecting precise geolocation data, and using dark patterns.

name: Online Children's Safety Protection Act
description: This bill establishes the Online Children's Safety Protection Act, requiring covered entities that provide an online service, product, or feature likely to be accessed by children to complete a data protection impact assessment determining whether the service is designed and offered consistent with the best interests of the child, and prohibits practices such as profiling, collecting precise geolocation data, and using dark patterns.

name: A5474
description: This bill protects the of minors featured in online video blogs (vlogs) by requiring online content creators who meet specified compensation and content thresholds to set aside a percentage of earnings tied to a minor's likeness or name in a trust preserved for the minor until reaching age 18. It also allows individuals who appeared as minors in such monetized content to request, upon reaching adulthood, the permanent deletion of those video segments from the relevant internet platform or content creator's account.

name: A4635
description: This bill protects the of minors featured in online video blogs (vlogs) by requiring online content creators who meet specified compensation and content thresholds to set aside a percentage of earnings tied to a minor's likeness or name in a trust preserved for the minor until reaching age 18. It also allows individuals who appeared as minors in such monetized content to request, upon reaching adulthood, the permanent deletion of those video segments from the relevant internet platform or content creator's account.

name: Age Verification, Parental Consent, Limited communications with minors via social media sites
description: This bill requires social media companies to verify the age of New Jersey account holders and obtain parent or guardian consent, including identity-verified consent, before allowing a minor to be an account holder, and prohibits social media platforms from permitting direct messaging between a minor's account and adult users not connected through mutual "adding." It also limits the collection of minors' personal information to what is necessary for legal compliance.

name: S1982
description: This bill requires social media companies to verify the age of New Jersey account holders and obtain parent or guardian consent, including identity-verified consent, before allowing a minor to be an account holder, and prohibits social media platforms from permitting direct messaging between a minor's account and adult users not connected through mutual "adding." It also limits the collection of minors' personal information to what is necessary for legal compliance.

name: S3993
description: This bill requires social media companies to verify the age of New Jersey account holders and obtain parent or guardian consent, including identity-verified consent, before allowing a minor to be an account holder, and prohibits social media platforms from permitting direct messaging between a minor's account and adult users not connected through mutual "adding." It also limits the collection of minors' personal information to what is necessary for legal compliance.

name: Digital Social Media Services and the Mental Health Care Fund
description: This bill establishes the Mental Health Care Fund for Children and Youth to improve access to mental health care services for children and youth in the State, and imposes a digital social media gross revenues tax, ranging from 5% to 10% depending on global annual gross revenues, on large social media services with the tax revenue distributed to the Fund.

name: Digital Social Media Services and the Mental Health Care Fund
description: This bill establishes the Mental Health Care Fund for Children and Youth to improve access to mental health care services for children and youth in the State, and imposes a digital social media gross revenues tax, ranging from 5% to 10% depending on global annual gross revenues, on large social media services with the tax revenue distributed to the Fund.

name: Digital Social Media Services and the Mental Health Care Fund
description: This bill establishes the Mental Health Care Fund for Children and Youth to improve access to mental health care services for children and youth in the State, and imposes a digital social media gross revenues tax, ranging from 5% to 10% depending on global annual gross revenues, on large social media services with the tax revenue distributed to the Fund.

name: Algorithmic Addiction Fund
description: This bill establishes the Algorithmic Addiction Fund to retain revenues recovered by the State from technology or social media companies, to be used for algorithmic addiction prevention, intervention, and treatment services, and requires the Governor to develop related goals and performance indicators and report annually to the General Assembly.

name: Algorithmic Addiction Fund
description: This bill establishes the Algorithmic Addiction Fund as a special, nonlapsing fund to retain settlement or judgment revenues recovered by the State from technology or social media companies, to be used for algorithmic addiction prevention, intervention, and treatment services, and requires the Governor to develop related goals and performance indicators and report annually to the General Assembly.

name: Social Media Platforms - Vloggers and Video Content Featuring Minors
description: This bill requires a vlogger who features a minor child in monetized video content posted on a social media platform to compensate the child by paying a percentage of gross earnings into a trust for the child. The bill also allows an adult who was featured as a minor in such content to request its permanent deletion and requires social media platforms to take reasonable steps to comply.

name: Child Influencers Protection Act
description: This bill requires a vlogger who features a minor child in monetized video content posted on a social media platform to compensate the child by paying a percentage of gross earnings into a trust for the child. The bill also allows an adult who was featured as a minor in such content to request its permanent deletion and requires social media platforms to take reasonable steps to comply.

name: Child Influencers Protection Act
description: This bill requires a vlogger who features a minor child in monetized video content posted on a social media platform to compensate the child by paying a percentage of gross earnings into a trust for the child. The bill also allows an adult who was featured as a minor in such content to request its permanent deletion and requires social media platforms to take reasonable steps to comply.

name: Maryland Kids' Code
description: This bill establishes the Maryland Age-Appropriate Design Code Act, requiring businesses that offer an online product likely to be accessed by children to complete a data protection impact assessment identifying risks to children before offering the product to the public, and prohibits practices such as profiling children by default, collecting precise geolocation data, and using dark patterns that circumvent privacy protections.

name: Maryland Kids' Code
description: This bill establishes the Maryland Age-Appropriate Design Code Act, requiring businesses that offer an online product likely to be accessed by children to complete a data protection impact assessment identifying risks to children before offering the product to the public, and prohibits practices such as profiling children by default, collecting precise geolocation data, and using dark patterns that circumvent privacy protections.

name: S3608
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using any practice, design, feature, or affordance that the platform knew or should have known would cause child users to become addicted to the platform

name: S1964
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using any practice, design, feature, or affordance that the platform knew or should have known would cause child users to become addicted to the platform

name: S4035
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using any practice, design, feature, or affordance that the platform knew or should have known would cause child users to become addicted to the platform

name: Human Trafficking and Child Exploitation Prevention Act
description: This bill prevents human trafficking, child exploitation, and the distribution of explicit material through the internet. It further ensures that all devices have active filters that blocks explicit material harmful to minors.

name: Personal Data
description: This bill requires a parent of a child under the age sixteen to provide verified consent in order for their child to create a social media account. Additionally this bill allows parent's to control their child’s usage time and preapprove any photos before they are posted.

name: Student Access to Internet and Wireless Devices
description: This bill restricts student access to social media platforms through school-provided internet. This bill also limits the use of wireless communication devices by students during the school day, except when devices are used for educational purposes.

name: Government Information Technology Use
description: This bill requires commercial entities that publish material on websites and social media platforms to implement age verification protocols to prevent minors under the age of 18 from accessing adult content online.

name: Parent Training
description: This bill requires the Department of Education to develop a webpage for parent training programs and resources on child sex trafficking and other internet crimes against children in grades K-8.

name: Social Media Protections
description: This bill requires social media platforms to enable default privacy settings, provide a feature to opt out of data collection beyond what is necessary, and prohibit targeted advertising for minors.

name: Healthy Social Media Use
description: This bill ensures a free resource bank is developed, which provides research-based, scholarly articles on the mental and physical health impacts of social media on youth. Additionally, the bill ensures student wellness programs in elementary and secondary schools include initiatives to address problems regarding technology use.

name: Concealing and Revealing Social Media Information
description: This bill requires social media companies to create and publish clear policies on their platforms. These policies must include contact information for user questions and a description of the process for flagging content that violates the policies. This bill also requires social media platform's to implement age verification and treat a user as a juvenile if the user's device signals that the user is a juvenile.

name: Require Age Checks for Online Sexual Materials
description: This bill requires websites that publish sexual content to implement age verification to prevent children under the age of 18 from accessing it.

name: Social Media Protection of Minors
description: This bill requires social media companies to disclose information about use of addictive design features, photo modifications, etc. Additionally, the bill requires social media companies to provide protective measures such as screen time and a disclaimer about how the application may be harmful to users' mental health.

name: Technology Transperancy
description: This bill prohibits the government from restricting social media content or accounts. This bill also protects children under the age of 18 in online spaces. Social media platforms are prohibited from processing data if it harms children, profiling children, using dark patterns, and collecting precise geolocation.

name: Childhood Mental Health,  Safety, and Welfare
description: This bill requires mental health professionals to complete a course on technology addiction and pornography addiction. Additionally, the Commission on Mental Health & Substance Abuse must develop recommendations for how schools and providers should identify, treat and serve children with technology and pornography addiction.

name: Technology in K-12 Public Schools
description: This bill focuses on technology use in K-12 public schools and prohibits the use of certain social media platforms such as TikTok on district-owned devices and through the internet for students.

name: Online Protections for Minors
description: This bill requires social media platforms to implement age verification for account holders. It also requires platforms to prevent minors under the age of 16 from creating new accounts, while also mandating termination options for existing minor accounts.

name: Online Protections for Minors
description: This bill requires social media platforms to bans accounts for children under 13 and required parental consent for children ages 14 and 15. Additionally, this bill requires age verification for online websites and platforms featuring content harmful to minors including sexually explicit content.

name: Social Media Protection of Minors
description: This bill prohibits K-12 schools from having social media accounts and requiring students to have social media accounts for educational purposes. Additionally, this bill requires social media platforms to provide local resources for suicide and domestic violence prevention and include protective measures for minors.

name: Public Records and Investigations
description: This bill makes information collected by the Florida Department of Legal Affairs during investigations of social media and age-verification violations confidential and exempt from public records while the investigation is active. After an investigation ends, sensitive data about minors such as names, identifying information, and personal details remains exempt from public records requests to prevent privacy violations and identity theft.

name: Protect Our Children Act
description: This bill requires manufacturers of tablets or smartphones to include a filter that blocks material harmful to minors under the age of 18 and is enabled upon activation of the device.

name: Social Media Use for Minors
description: This bill requires social media platforms to prohibit minors under the age of 16 from creating new accounts and implement methods for age verification.

name: Social Media Use By Minors
description: This bill requires social media platforms to permanently delete personal information within 45 days of account termination, allow confirmed parents or legal guardians of minor account holders to view a list of account names with which the minor has exchanged messages, disclose certain data to law enforcement upon valid legal process related to a criminal investigation, and prohibit minor account holders from using or accessing messages designed to disappear or self-destruct after a certain period.

name: Social Media Use By Minors
description: This bill requires mandatory termination for minors under the age of 14 and permits parents to terminate their child’s account. This bill prohibits a minor account holder from using or accessing messages that are designed to disappear. This bill requires social media platforms to provide a mechanism to decrypt end-to-end encryption so law enforcement can view messages.

name: Social Media Protection for Minors
description: This bill prohibits K-12 schools from using or having an account on certain social media platforms. Additionally, schools can’t require students to register, enroll, or participate in social media platforms for educational purposes.

name: Standard Based Media Literacy Curriculum
description: This bill requires schools to include media literacy within K-12 school cirriculum and educate students about misinformation and online hate speech.

name: Schools Media Literacy Curriculum
description: This bill requires schools to include media literacy within K-12 school cirriculum and educate student about misinformation and online hate speech.

name: Minors Addictive Content Protections
description: This bill protects minors under the age of 18 from addictive content by imposing special requirements for operators regarding consent, notifications, and age verification.

name: Minors Internet Safety
description: This bill requires any commercial entity that knowingly or intentionally distributes harmful material, including sexually explicit content, to minors under the age of 18 to implement age verification.

name: App Store Child Safety
description: This bill requires app store providers to verify user’s age before they download an app and prevent minors under the age of 18 from downloading apps unsuitable for their age category. This also requires app store providers to  provide a mechanism for parents to block minors from downloading unsuitable apps.

name: Online Safety for Children
description: This bill requires businesses that provide an online service to enable default privacy settings, not use dark patterns, and not collect unnecessary data from children under the age of 18

name: Study Article of School Code
description: This bill requires internet education curriculum for K-12 schools to be updated every 3 years to ensure the curriculum is up to date.

name: Children's Privacy Protection and Parental Empowerment Act
description: This bill requires businesses that provide online services used by minors under the age of 18 to implement protections such as default privacy settings.

name: Child Labor Laws
description: This bill establishes protections and compensation requirements for minors in vlog content. This ensures once the minor turns 18 they have the right to delete their content. This bill also ensures that guardians of vloggers must set aside minors earnings in a trust account.

name: Minors and Social Media Harmful Algorithms
description: This bill urges the federal government to create regulations that promote well-being of minors and protect them from harmful social media algorithms.

name: School Code
description: This bill prohibits students enrolled in public schools from using personal wireless communication devices during instructional time and prevents them from accessing social media platforms through school-provided internet.

name: SCH CD- Digital Literacy Skills
description: This bill requires all school districts to ensure that students receive opportunities to gain digital literacy skills, such as digital citizenship skills, media literacy, and AI literacy, beginning in elementary school.

name: Parental Digital Choice Act
description: This bill requires social media platforms to allow third-party safety software so parents can protect their children under the age of 18 from certain harms including cyberbullying, human trafficking and illegal drug distribution.

name: Child Labor-Trust Account
description: This bill requires vloggers to set aside a specified amount of gross earnings from video content featuring their minor child under the age of 16 into a trust account to be obtained when the minor reaches adulthood.

name: Monitoring Child Online
description: This bill requires social media platforms to allow third-party safety software so parents can monitor the children's online interactions.

name: Age-Appropriate Design Code
description: This bill requires all covered entities that operate in the state and process children's data to do so in a manner consistent with the best interests of children under the age of 18. It also enables default privacy settings.

name: Parental Consent Social Media
description: This bill prohibits social media companies from permitting a user who is a minor under the age of 18 to be an account holder unless the minor has provided parental consent. Additionally, social media companies must implement a curfew prohibiting minors from accessing the platform between 10 PM and 6 AM.

name: Social Media Algorithms
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from using algorithms on accounts owned by minors under the age of 18.

name: Age Appropriate Design Act
description: This bill requires online platforms to prioritize the privacy, safety, and well-being of children under the age of 18 over their commercial interests. The bill prohibits practices such as profiling children by default or processing precise geolocation data.

name: Consumer Protection
description: This bill prohibits social media algorithms from targeting children under the age of 18. Additionally, the bill requires minors to obtain permission from their legal guardian prior to opening a new user account.

name: Certain social media algorithms that target children prohibited
description: This bill prohibits certain social media algorithms and recommendation features that target children under the age of 18.

name: Minor access to chatbots for recreational purposes by persons prohibition provision
description: This bill prohibits platforms from allowing minors under the age of 18 to access chatbots for recreational purposes. Additionally, platforms with chatbots for recreational purposes must implement age verification.

name: Minors Appearing in Internet Content Creation Provided Compensation
description: This bill requires content creators whose video content features a minor to maintain records and set aside a portion of earnings into a trust account preserved for the minor until age 18, while prohibiting minors under 14 from engaging in content creation .The bill also allows minors and adults who appeared as minors to request removal of content featuring their likeness.

name: Use of Social Media for Minors
description: This bill requires social media companies to implement age verification to restrict access for minors under the age of 15.

name: Digital Citizenship
description:
This bill establishes the Digital Citizenship, Internet Safety, and Media Literacy Advisory Council.
The advisory council will make reccomendations for best instuctional practices regarding how students in K-12 school can safely use media and technology resources.

name: Media Literacy Committee
description: The bill focuses on creating a media literacy advisory group which reviews media literacy education within school districts.
notes: Failed, Reintroduced in 2025 cycle as A120

name: Media Literacy Committee
description: The bill focuses on creating a media literacy advisory group which reviews media literacy education within school districts.

name: Media Literacy Committee
description: This bill establishes a Media Literacy Committee that evaluates best practices, instructional resources, and educational models for effectively teaching media literacy to students within school districts.
notes: Failed, Reintroduced in 2025 cycle as A150

name: Media Literacy Committee
description: This bill establishes a Media Literacy Committee that evaluates best practices, instructional resources, and educational models for effectively teaching media literacy to students within school districts.

name: Child Influencer Protection
description: This bill amends New York's labor law definitions of "artistic or creative services" and "child performer" to include children who participate in videos posted to video-sharing or social networking websites that generate earnings, thereby extending existing child performer protections to these children.

name: Interactive Computer Service Providers
description: The bill prevents computer service providers from promoting content that may cause physical or psychological harm to minors under the age of 18. Additionally, platforms are prohibited from targeting minors using personal data such as location, medical status, race, gender, age, or relationships to promote harmful or injurious content.

name: New York child data privacy and protection act
description: This bill strengthens data privacy protections for minors under the age of 18. Platforms must use “privacy by default” settings, prohibits targeted advertising and certain data collection.

name: Age Verification
description: This bill requires social media networks to verify the age of new and existing account holders, using a third-party vendor, within 30 days of account opening or first access after the effective date, and to deny access to accounts that fail to complete verification within that period.

name: SAFE for Kids
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from providing  addictive, algorithm-based feeds to minors under the age of 18.

name: Online Products Targeted Towards Children
description: This bill requires online products targeted towards children under the age of 18 to incorporate features allowing parents or guardians to set screen time limits for minors. Additionally, the bill prohibits content that promotes harmful or illegal activities.

name: Social Media Monitoring Act
description: This bill provides data analytic resources and funding to every school district to allow the monitoring of social media activities to provide early-detection information of possible threats to a student's health and the safety of the school.

name: Parent Consent
description: This bill requires protective features to safeguard children under the age of 13. This bill requires parent access to the personal information and content collected about their child.

name: Regulation of Social Media Companies and Platforms
description: This bill, the New York Social Media Regulation Act, prohibits social media companies from permitting a minor to hold an account without parental consent, requires age and consent verification, and restricts minor accounts by banning direct messaging with unconnected users, advertising, targeted content, and most data collection, while giving parents password access to view their minor's posts and messages. The bill also limits minor account access to between 6:30 a.m. and 10:30 p.m. (subject to parental adjustment)

name: Statewide Social Media and Mental Health Awareness Campaign
description: This bill establishes statewide youth mental health and social media campaign promote public awareness of the impacts of social media on mental health. The campaign will educate people on techniques to reduce feelings of isolation, anxiety, sleep quality which can be exacerbated by social media.

name: Statewide Social Media and Mental Health Awareness Campaign
description: This bill establishes statewide youth mental health and social media campaign promote public awareness of the impacts of social media on mental health. The campaign will educate people on techniques to reduce feelings of isolation, anxiety, sleep quality which can be exacerbated by social media.

name: Media Literacy Standards
description: This bill creates a set of media literacy standards for students in grades kindergarten through twelve, which focuses on internet safety and digital citizenship.

name: Protections for Minors Featured in Influencer-Generated Content
description: This bill establishes protections, such as trust accounts for minors under the age of 18 who are featured in influencer-generated content that generates earnings from being shared on social media.

name: Media Literacy Standards for K-12
description: This bill requires the Board of Regents and Commissioner of Education to incorporate a media literacy component into instruction for students in kindergarten through twelfth grade, covering internet safety, civility, and digital citizenship, and establishes a set of media literacy education standards covering skills such as evaluating digital sources, managing one's digital footprint, protecting personal privacy, and combating cyberbullying.

name: Warning Labels for Social Media
description: This bill requires operators of "addictive social media platforms" (those offering features like an addictive feed, push notifications, autoplay, infinite scroll, or like counts) to display a warning label to all users each time they access the platform, and prohibits design features like burying the label in terms of service or otherwise obscuring or undermining it.

name: Stop Addictive Feeds Exploitation (SAFE)
description: This bill prohibits social media platforms from providing an addictive social media feed to a user unless the platform offers methods to turn off algorithmic recommendations. Additionally, the bill prohibits the use of dark patterns on children.

name: Regulation of Social Media Companies
description: This bill prohibits social media companies from permitting a user who is a minor under the age of 18 to be an account holder unless the minor has provided parental consent.
